commands.review_summary:
Adds an Android Settings > Cron Jobs detail screen that fetches `cron.get`, renders read-only job fields, makes the cron job id copyable, and raises the Android cron list fetch limit from 20 to 100.

PR surface: Other +462. Total +462 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. Source inspection shows current main is Android list-only, and the inspected screenshots show the after-change list, detail, copy, and back flows.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is Android maintainer UX review plus exact-head CI for a new user-facing feature.

target.title: feat(android): show cron job details
target.body:
No linked issue. This is a narrow Android settings usability improvement.

## What Problem This Solves

Android users could see the Cron Jobs settings summary and list, but tapping a cron job did not open the job itself. That made it hard to inspect the schedule, payload, delivery state, or copy the job id when discussing a cron job in chat or support.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an Android-only cron job detail view that uses the existing `cron.get` gateway method. The view stays read-only: it does not add cron editing, new gateway methods, protocol changes, auth scopes, or command actions.

The cron job id row is now copyable, with a copy affordance and a toast, so users can quickly reference the exact job id elsewhere.

## User Impact

Users can now open `Settings > Cron Jobs`, tap a scheduled job, inspect the gateway-provided details, copy the cron job id, refresh the detail view, and use either the toolbar back button or Android system back to return to the Cron Jobs list.

## Evidence

Redacted local Android emulator proof against a running OpenClaw gateway:

| View | What it proves | Proof |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cron Jobs list | Cron jobs render as tappable rows with status and chevrons. Private cron names/prompts are redacted. | ![Cron Jobs list](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tosko4/openclaw/7ba5a7fa1535238bf81db792c426b2d57e8cc9dd/proof/android-cron-detail/cron-list-redacted.png) |
| Cron job detail | Tapping a row opens the read-only `cron.get` detail view. Private title/id are redacted. | ![Cron job detail](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tosko4/openclaw/7ba5a7fa1535238bf81db792c426b2d57e8cc9dd/proof/android-cron-detail/cron-detail-redacted.png) |
| Copy id affordance | The ID row exposes a copy icon and `Tap to copy`; Android clipboard preview is redacted. | ![Copy cron id](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tosko4/openclaw/7ba5a7fa1535238bf81db792c426b2d57e8cc9dd/proof/android-cron-detail/cron-copy-redacted.png) |
| System back | Android system back from detail returns to the Cron Jobs list instead of Settings home. Private cron names/prompts are redacted. | ![System back returns to list](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Tosko4/openclaw/7ba5a7fa1535238bf81db792c426b2d57e8cc9dd/proof/android-cron-detail/cron-system-back-redacted.png) |

Validation run after rebasing onto current `upstream/main`:

```text
ANDROID_HOME=/home/nabu/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testThirdPartyDebugUnitTest --no-daemon --stacktrace
ANDROID_HOME=/home/nabu/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:assembleThirdPartyDebug --no-daemon --stacktrace
git diff --check
/home/nabu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main
```

All four checks passed.

Additional note: `:app:ktlintMainSourceSetCheck` was attempted, but it is currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated ktlint violations in `ChatController.kt` and `ChatScreen.kt` that are outside this Android cron detail patch.

AI-assisted: implemented and validated with Codex, then manually inspected and tested in a local Android/OpenClaw setup.

commands.author: Tosko4

target.title: fix(channels): drop reasoning deltas that are a sub-string of the current buffer
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `mergeReasoningProgressText` in `src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.ts` only guarded the case where an incoming reasoning delta *extends* the current buffer (`normalizedIncoming.startsWith(normalizedCurrent)`, the snapshot/continuation path). It did not cover the reverse direction — an incoming delta that is a *sub-string* of the current buffer.
- **Root cause:** A partial resend (e.g. after a network hiccup or reconnect) where `current = "Reading files"` and `incoming = "Read"` is normalized such that `normalizedCurrent.startsWith(normalizedIncoming)` is true, but the snapshot check uses the opposite direction. The delta fell through to the append branch and concatenated already-seen text, producing `"Reading filesRead"` — a duplicated prefix in the streaming progress draft.
- **Fix:** Add the symmetric check: when `current` already starts with the incoming normalized text, keep the longer `current` buffer and wait for the next delta instead of duplicating the prefix.
- **Impact:** All `mode === "progress"` channels' `pushReasoningProgress` path (`reasoningRawText` buffer). No behavior change for valid append deltas, equal deltas, or snapshot extensions.

## Linked context

N/A (no existing issue)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Streaming reasoning progress draft duplicated already-displayed text when an incoming reasoning delta was a sub-string of the current buffer (partial resend / reconnect retry), e.g. `Reading files` + `Read` rendered as `Reading filesRead`.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v24.14.0, commit `ec692c95c1` on branch `fix/channels-reasoning-substring-dedup`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node` runtime verification script that imports the patched `createChannelProgressDraftCompositor` directly (no mocks), drives `pushReasoningProgress` with each delta sequence, and asserts the last rendered reasoning line. Command: `node --import tsx /tmp/verify-reasoning-substring.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture of `node` runtime verification (copied live output):
```
[PASS] B1 substring resend: Reading files + Read
         rendered reasoning = "_Reading files_"
[PASS] B3 longer substring: ...something + ...and then
         rendered reasoning = "_Reading files and then something_"
[PASS] B4 prefix resend: Hello world + Hel
         rendered reasoning = "_Hello world_"
[PASS] D sanity snapshot extension: Reading + Reading files (keep extension)
         rendered reasoning = "_Reading files_"
[PASS] E sanity equal: Reading + Reading
         rendered reasoning = "_Reading_"
[PASS] A sanity append deltas: Reading +  files (concatenate)
         rendered reasoning = "_Reading files_"

ALL PASS
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The sub-string resend deltas (`B1`, `B3`, `B4`) no longer duplicate the buffer; the longer `current` text is retained. Sanity paths still hold: snapshot extension keeps the extension, equal deltas are unchanged, and normal delta append still concatenates correctly.
- **What was not tested:** Live provider reconnect over a real network with a production LLM stream. The fix is unit- and runtime-verified at the compositor boundary where the merge logic lives.

## Tests and validation

```
$ pnpm test src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)

$ pnpm build
[build-all] write-cli-startup-metadata done in 32.2s
 phase timings: total 643.5s
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] One concern per PR — only the reasoning merge dedup branch + one regression test.
- [x] No config/env/CLI surface changes.
- [x] No plugin/provider/auth/ownership boundary changes.
- [x] Backward compatible — valid append deltas, equal deltas, and snapshot extensions are unchanged.
- [x] Added a test covering the bug (`ignores reasoning deltas that are a substring of the current buffer`).
- [x] `pnpm build` passes.
- [x] No `CHANGELOG.md` edit.

- [x] Allow edits by maintainers
